title: Asuddenriseineuropeanfootballsoccermatchattendancepredictsincreaseddiscretionaryspendingintheconsumerdiscretionarysector
thesis: Higher match attendance reflects increased consumer confidence and leisure spending willingness in Europe, often spilling over to U.S. consumer discretionary stocks. Rising leisure and entertainment engagement abroad tends to correlate with improved discretionary spending trends influencing related U.S. companies.
signal_logic: If average match attendance for top 5 European leagues rises >10% week-over-week
entry_exit: Entry: If average match attendance for top 5 European leagues rises >10% week-over-week Exit: After 15 trading days or if attendance growth reverses
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use UEFA official match attendance reports via scrape (weekly).

schema.idea: A sudden rise in European football (soccer) match attendance predicts increased discretionary spending in the consumer discretionary sector.
schema.behavior: Higher match attendance reflects increased consumer confidence and leisure spending willingness in Europe, often spilling over to U.S. consumer discretionary stocks.

market_impact.rationale: Rising leisure and entertainment engagement abroad tends to correlate with improved discretionary spending trends influencing related U.S. companies.

trade_logic.entry: If average match attendance for top 5 European leagues rises >10% week-over-week
trade_logic.exit: After 15 trading days or if attendance growth reverses
